note: Rescores the currently persisted accuracy-report/blind_findings.jsonl against the current detector. This is a deterministic replay/rescore of saved findings, not an attacker-generation run; after eval/blind_redteam.mjs it reflects the fresh live-run corpus.

false_positives.interpretation: The artifact shows an active SMB session terminating on this host's port 445 from internal peer 10.3.58.5; consistent with normal Windows file-sharing traffic between two hosts on the 10.3.58.0 subnet.
false_positives.reason: Cited token(s) NOT present in the tool output as a standalone token (hallucinated/unsupported): 10.3.58.0.
false_positives.note: Restates connection tuple, state, and PID exactly as shown; SMB is the registered service for 445, stays within what netscan reveals.

false_negatives.interpretation: PID 720 was the svchost.exe service host that spawned both the McAfee manager and the WMI provider, confirming a standard service-hosting chain.
false_negatives.note: PID 720 appears only as a PPID; no row for PID 720 exists in the output, so naming it 'svchost.exe' is a fabricated token/identity.
